topic: AI lessons from the boardroom: Daan Debie on how NS brings AI from pilot to practice
post_original:
Getting AI out of the pilot phase requires more than a good model. 🤔

Daan Debie, Director of Data, Innovation and AI at NS, shared how the railway company has brought AI into everyday operations -- from predicting crowdedness on trains to estimating bike availability at stations.

The technical solutions matter, but Debie was clear: governance and comprehensive staff training are what make AI stick. Deploying a system is one thing. Equipping your people to work with it confidently is another.

NS also involved end users from day one, which helped ensure the tools actually served the people using them -- not just the teams building them.

🚉 The result is AI that operates reliably at scale, in one of the Netherlands' most complex logistical environments.

At KickstartAI, we see this pattern across the ecosystem: organizations that invest in governance and people alongside technology are the ones moving fastest.

👉 Read more about how NS brings AI from pilot to practice: https://kickstart.ai/news/daan-debie-ns-how-ns-brings-ai-from-pilot-to-practice

#AIadoption #KickstartAI #ResponsibleAI #NetherlandsAI #AIgovernance

topic: Agentic Engineering II: Best practices to avoid slop and actually improve productivity with coding agents
post_original:
Adopting AI in your development process is exciting -- and it comes with real responsibilities around quality and control. 🎯

Our new blog on coding agent best practices speaks directly to this. LLMs with tool access can autonomously decide actions and adapt based on outcomes, which makes them powerful. That same autonomy is exactly why structured practices matter so much.

Test-driven development, gradual context expansion, and consistent human review are what separate productive AI-assisted workflows from ones that quietly accumulate technical debt.

With frameworks like the EU AI Act shaping how organizations in the Netherlands approach AI, building reliable habits from the start is a sound long-term investment. Responsible adoption and high productivity go hand in hand -- and our blog shows what that looks like in practice. 💡

Read the full piece via the link below.

https://kickstart.ai/news/agentic-engineering-best-practices-coding-agents-avoid-slop-increase-productivity

#ResponsibleAI #AIAct #CodingAgents #AIAdoption #KickstartAI

topic: Agentic Engineering I: A non-technical guide to working with coding agents
post_original:
Coding agents can do a lot -- and that's exactly why adopting them thoughtfully matters. ⚖️

Unlike a standard chat assistant, these AI systems act autonomously over longer timeframes, making decisions and executing tasks in sequence. The productivity gains are real: teams can automate repetitive work and redirect their focus toward problems that genuinely need human insight.

At the same time, speed without guardrails creates risk. Scope creep is a common pitfall -- when an agent moves fast, the impulse to keep expanding its tasks can outpace the team's ability to test and maintain what's been built.

Responsible adoption means putting clear boundaries in place from the start and building habits around reviewing what the agent produces. The EU AI Act reinforces why accountability in AI systems matters, and coding agents are no exception. 🇪🇺

At KickstartAI, we believe the Dutch ecosystem is well-positioned to lead on exactly this balance: embracing powerful AI tools while keeping human oversight at the center of the process.

Our latest article walks through what that looks like in practice -- written for non-technical readers as much as engineers. 🙌

👉 Link in the comments.

#ResponsibleAI #CodingAgents #EUAIAct #AIInnovation #KickstartAI

topic: KLM CIO Aart Slagt on why an AI model only has value if users understand and trust it
post_original:
Moving AI out of the pilot phase and into real operations is one of the hardest steps any organization faces. 🚀

KLM CIO Aart Slagt recently shared how the airline made exactly that move -- shifting from broad experimentation toward a focused deployment strategy, with AI now embedded in areas like planning and optimization.

Two projects stand out as proof points: a catering prediction model that reduces waste, and a contingency fuel model that sharpens operational decisions. Both deliver measurable value, and both required something beyond good data science -- they required users who actually understand and trust the models they work with.

That last part is easy to underestimate. An AI model only creates value when the people using it believe in it enough to act on its output. KLM addressed this head-on by building Talia, a secure environment where employees can engage with AI tools safely and confidently.

This is the kind of responsible, grounded approach that moves the needle -- not just for one airline, but for AI adoption across the Dutch ecosystem. 🇳🇱

Read the full story via the link in the comments 👇

#KickstartAI #AIadoption #ResponsibleAI #AIinNetherlands #DigitalTransformation

topic: Why managing GenAI projects requires a playbook tweak
post_original:
GenAI projects follow their own rules -- and experienced teams are rewriting their playbooks accordingly. 📋

Through our collaboration with ING on a GenAI-powered financial health assistant, KickstartAI gained firsthand insight into what makes these projects different from traditional software development.

Three shifts stand out:

- Expectation alignment on how freely the model can respond shapes everything downstream.
- Iterative, high-level scoping keeps cross-functional teams moving in the same direction without over-engineering early decisions.
- Evaluation and maintenance must be designed for scale from the start, especially in sectors where the EU AI Act sets clear accountability standards.

Financial services is one of the highest-stakes environments for deploying AI responsibly. Getting the process right matters as much as getting the technology right.

We are proud to be building this knowledge together with partners like ING and sharing it openly with the broader Dutch AI community. 🤝

Find the full article linked in the comments. 👇

#GenAI #AIProjects #FinancialHealth #EUAIAct #KickstartAI

topic: Why focus and technology go hand in hand: ING's Leon Dusée
post_original:
AI expertise should reach every corner of the economy -- not just the organizations with the largest budgets. 🌐

Leon Dusée, Global Head COO Risk at ING, sees real potential in initiatives like KickstartAI to spread practical AI knowledge across sectors that might otherwise struggle to access it. That kind of democratization is exactly where we want to have an impact.

His broader message is just as relevant: successful AI adoption comes down to three things:

🔸 Choosing a clear focus instead of pursuing every opportunity at once.
🔸 Redesigning processes so AI genuinely adds value.
🔸 Preparing teams to think critically about the tools they use.

These are the building blocks of an AI strategy that holds up -- in a startup, a hospital, a public institution, or a global bank.

We are grateful to leaders like Dusée for sharing their experience openly. Progress in the Dutch AI ecosystem is a shared achievement. 🤝

Read the full article via the link below 👇
https://kickstart.ai/news/why-focus-ai-technology-go-hand-in-hand-ing

topic: Inside the Police's AI approach to environmental crime
post_original:
Environmental crime cases are notoriously hard to crack. 🌿

Layers of legislation, scattered case law, and long initial analysis phases slow investigators down before a case even gets moving.

Together with the Dutch National Police, KickstartAI developed Al Capone -- an AI tool designed to help investigators navigate legal frameworks and find relevant precedents faster during the early stages of an environmental crime case.

Senior innovation advisor Amir Niknam, who is leading the initiative at the Police, puts it well: AI should be a means, not a strategy. The tool supports human judgment rather than replacing it, and that distinction matters enormously in law enforcement.

Explainability and human oversight are baked into the design, keeping the approach aligned with EU AI regulation and, more importantly, with the rule of law. ⚖️

Efficient investigations mean faster accountability -- and that has real consequences for sustainability and environmental protection in the Netherlands.

👇 Read the full interview with Amir Niknam on our website:
https://kickstart.ai/news/inside-police-ai-approach-environmental-crime

#AI #EnvironmentalCrime #ResponsibleAI #KickstartAI #PublicSector

He believes AI works best when it supports the judgment of investigators, and the tool is designed with exactly that principle in mind. topic: Why (and how) we need to measure data diversity in language models
post_original:
What does it actually mean for a dataset to be diverse? 🤔

We use the word often, but measuring diversity in language model training data is surprisingly hard. Stylistic diversity -- how text is written -- and semantic diversity -- what topics and perspectives it covers -- require different tools and different thinking.

Dong Nguyen, associate professor at Utrecht University, is building those tools through the DataDivers project. The research works toward concrete metrics that researchers and practitioners can use to evaluate and improve the datasets behind today's AI systems.

More systematic dataset construction could help the AI community build models that generalize better and serve broader populations more fairly. In a landscape where AI deployment is accelerating across sectors, getting the foundations right is essential.

At KickstartAI, we believe this kind of rigorous, purpose-driven research is central to an AI ecosystem that works for everyone in the Netherlands and beyond. 🌍

Read our full conversation with Dong Nguyen below.
https://kickstart.ai/news/why-how-measure-data-diversity-language-models

#DataDiversity #LLMs #AIResearch #EthicalAI #KickstartAI

topic: Building a financial health chatbot with GenAI: The ING Challenge
post_original:
🚀 Two weeks. Fourteen participants. One shared challenge: making financial guidance accessible to the people who need it most.

The KickstartAI x ING Financial Health Chatbot challenge brought together experts from across our partner network to co-create a GenAI-powered tool for Dutch households facing financial stress or uncertainty. Around 47% of households in the Netherlands experience some form of financial vulnerability -- a figure that makes this kind of initiative genuinely urgent.

What made the challenge work was the depth of collaboration. Teams combined expertise in AI architecture, financial education, ethics, and regulatory compliance to produce a prototype ready for validation and potential scaling.

Cross-sector challenges like this one are at the heart of how KickstartAI accelerates AI adoption. When organizations build together rather than in silos, the solutions that emerge are more robust, more human, and more ready for the real world. 🤝

We are grateful to ING and every participant who brought their knowledge and energy to this initiative. The Netherlands has the talent and the ambition -- projects like this one show what that combination can achieve.

topic: How agentic AI can bridge the data-business gap
post_original:
What if anyone in your organization could ask a data question and get a real answer -- without waiting for a data analyst? 💡

That's the promise behind agentic AI for data analysis. In our latest Community Spotlight, Data Scientist Stan Dumont of Lynxx shares how he experimented with agentic AI to let non-technical users interact with data in plain language, skipping the manual bottlenecks that slow most teams down.

A few things stood out from the conversation:

🔸 Natural language interaction changes who can access insights -- and how quickly.
🔸 Architecture choices matter: some frameworks add friction, while others make the workflow genuinely efficient.
🔸 Accuracy is measurable, but agentic AI still requires thoughtful guardrails to be trustworthy in practice.

Making data more accessible across an organization is exactly the kind of shift that moves the needle on AI adoption -- and keeps it grounded in real business value.

👇 Read the full story here:
https://kickstart.ai/news/how-agentic-ai-bridge-data-business-gap

#AI #AgenticAI #DataDemocratization #AIAdoption #NetherlandsAI

topic: How Earth observation and AI are opening up space data
post_original:
🌱 The Netherlands faces some of Europe's most complex environmental challenges -- and AI is becoming a serious tool for addressing them.

Combining satellite imagery with AI-driven analysis, local governments can now explore housing trends, monitor water quality, and model future scenarios with far greater confidence. These are decisions that affect real communities, and getting them right requires the best available information.

Startups in the Dutch ecosystem are already proving the concept. By using publicly available space data alongside AI, they are delivering environmental assessments that support sustainable planning and help quantify ecological impact.

This is exactly the kind of applied AI adoption KickstartAI exists to accelerate -- practical, purposeful, and grounded in real societal needs. The technology is ready. The data is accessible. The opportunity for the Dutch ecosystem is now. 🤝

👇 Explore the full article on how AI is reshaping Earth observation:
https://kickstart.ai/news/how-earth-observation-ai-opening-space-data

#AI #ClimateAction #UrbanPlanning #DutchInnovation #Sustainability

topic: Community Spotlight: Building offline AI teachers with Dr Pauldy Otermans & Dev Aditya
post_original:
🎙️ In our latest Community Spotlight, we spoke with two founders doing quietly remarkable work.

Dr. Pauldy Otermans and Dev Aditya of the Otermans Institute began with a clear ambition: upskill 750 million learners by 2030. They built Beatrice, the world's first AI teacher, launched her in 2021, and have since expanded to 19 countries -- with a focus on marginalized communities across Africa and beyond.

Their journey covers a lot of ground:

- Deploying AI teachers in refugee camps and underserved schools.
- Partnering with governments and institutions to scale responsibly.
- Engineering an offline mode so learners without reliable internet can still access high-quality education.

What stands out is how deliberately they have approached each step -- keeping ethics, privacy, and real community needs at the center of every decision.

KickstartAI is proud to share stories like this one. Accelerating AI adoption means lifting up the builders who are using AI to create genuine societal value. 🤝

Read the full spotlight here 👇
https://kickstart.ai/news/pauldy-otermans-dev-aditya-building-offline-ai-teachers

#AI #CommunitySpotlight #AIforGood #KickstartAI #AIinEducation

topic: Fuel planning optimization with machine learning: the KLM challenge
post_original:
Great things happen when organizations pool their expertise 🤝

KickstartAI recently wrapped up Daidalos, a machine learning challenge with KLM focused on optimizing fuel planning in aviation. Twelve data scientists from multiple organizations spent two intensive weeks digging into one of aviation's most complex operational problems: how to plan contingency fuel more accurately.

A few highlights from the project:

- The LightGBM model delivered strong results after carefully refining the input data.
- Weather data had less predictive impact than initially expected -- a finding that sharpens where future efforts should focus.
- Explainability tools were developed so pilots and dispatchers can understand and trust the model's output.

Trust is often the real bottleneck in AI adoption, and building it into the solution from the start made a real difference here.

The next step is validating the solution with business stakeholders before moving toward implementation. We're proud to be part of this journey with KLM and the talented people who made it happen. 🚀

#AI #Aviation #MachineLearning #Collaboration #DutchEcosystem

topic: A conversation with Erick Webbe, KickstartAI's new CEO
post_original:
The Netherlands has every ingredient for AI leadership: top-tier research, a strong digital infrastructure, and companies already putting AI to work in meaningful ways. 🇳🇱

Yet a persistent focus on risk has slowed adoption at a moment when the rest of the world is accelerating. Our incoming CEO, Erick Webbe, puts it plainly: the priority now is demonstrating the value AI can deliver -- concretely, responsibly, and at scale.

KickstartAI partners are already doing exactly that. Airlines are using AI to streamline operations. Retailers are cutting food waste through smarter forecasting. Public transport providers are improving reliability for millions of daily commuters. ✅

These examples matter because they move AI from an abstract debate into something people can see and feel.

Erick will lead KickstartAI's efforts to connect organizations, share what works, and advocate for a national policy environment that supports responsible AI adoption. We are proud to have someone with his vision stepping into this role.

The full interview with Erick is linked in the comments 👇

#KickstartAI #AIAdoption #AINetherlands #Innovation #DutchAI

topic: Exploring multimodal AI models for biodiversity
post_original:
🦎 Identifying individual animals by the patterns on their skin. Forecasting biodiversity shifts using climate and species data. These are real AI applications being developed right now in the Netherlands.

TNO's BioAnalyst model and LIACS researcher Rita Pucci's TATTOOs project both demonstrate how multimodal AI -- models that learn from several types of data at once -- can open new doors for biodiversity research and conservation.

Biodiversity loss is accelerating globally, and researchers need better tools to track, understand, and respond to ecosystem changes. AI models that fuse diverse data sources can provide exactly that kind of nuanced, large-scale insight.

For Dutch policymakers and research institutions working within EU frameworks, advances like these arrive at exactly the right moment. 🌍

We sat down with the researchers behind both projects to understand how they work and where they are headed.

👇 Read the article here:
https://kickstart.ai/news/exploring-multimodal-ai-models-biodiversity

🌿 How can multimodal AI help us better understand biodiversity?

In our latest article, we explore two projects pushing that question forward: BioAnalyst, a large-scale foundation model developed at TNO for biodiversity forecasting, and the TATTOOs project at LIACS, which uses computer vision to identify individual animals by their unique skin patterns.

We spoke with the researchers behind both projects about how these models are built, what makes multimodal learning so valuable for ecosystem research, and where the work is headed next.
